items.title: Beyond Independent Frames: Latent Attention Masked Autoencoders for Multi-View Echocardiography
items.abstract: This paper introduces the Latent Attention Masked Autoencoder (LAMAE), a foundation model designed to overcome the limitations of frame-independent processing in multi-view echocardiography. LAMAE incorporates a latent attention module to facilitate information exchange across different frames and views directly in the latent space. This enables the model to learn a holistic representation of cardiac function by effectively aggregating variable-length, heterogeneous multi-view data.
items.figure_caption: LAMAE architecture overview . During pretraining ( left ), masked frames from multiple views are encoded and fused through the Latent Attention (LA) module to learn shared representations, which are used to reconstruct full frames. During finetuning ( right ), frames are processed through that same encoder and LA module, followed by a lightweight classification head.

items.title: Robustness of Vision Foundation Models to Common Perturbations
items.abstract: This paper presents the first systematic study on the robustness of vision foundation models to common image perturbations like JPEG compression and brightness changes. The authors introduce three robustness metrics, analyze their mathematical properties, and evaluate six industry models, finding them generally non-robust. Crucially, they demonstrate that these perturbations degrade downstream task performance and propose a fine-tuning method to improve robustness without sacrificing utility.
items.figure_caption: An example for the worst-robustness property.

items.title: An Analysis of Regularization and Fokker-Planck Residuals in Diffusion Models for Image Generation
items.abstract: This paper analyzes the trade-off between regularization methods and the Fokker-Planck (FP) equation residuals in diffusion models for image generation. The authors empirically investigate lightweight regularizers as alternatives to direct FP penalty terms, showing that simpler penalties can achieve similar benefits in reducing FP residuals and improving sample quality at a substantially lower computational cost.
items.figure_caption: Noise injection in the forward process following ( 1 ) (top) and image generation from noise according to ( 4 ) (bottom).

items.title: OmniLight: One Model to Rule All Lighting Conditions
items.abstract: This paper introduces **OmniLight**, a generalized model designed to handle diverse adverse lighting conditions like shadows and irregular illumination. It achieves this by extending a specialized baseline (DINOLight) with a novel **Wavelet Domain Mixture-of-Experts (WD-MoE)** architecture. The core contribution is creating a single, robust model capable of performing both shadow removal and adaptive lighting normalization across multiple, varied datasets.

items.title: UniDoc-RL: Coarse-to-Fine Visual RAG with Hierarchical Actions and Dense Rewards
items.abstract: UniDoc-RL proposes a unified reinforcement learning framework for visual RAG, enabling a Large Vision-Language Model agent to sequentially acquire visual evidence through coarse-to-fine actions like document retrieval, image selection, and region cropping. This hierarchical approach allows the model to suppress irrelevant content and focus on information-dense regions. The core contribution is the integration of these steps with a dense multi-reward scheme for effective end-to-end training, leading to superior visual reasoning.
items.figure_caption: Three critical factors for Visual RAG. UniDoc-RL address these challenges through the (a) Precise Selection action to bridge the semantic gap between coarse retrieval and reasoning, and an (b) Active Visual Perception action to focus on information-dense regions, both optimized via a (c) Dense multi-reward mechanism.

items.title: Why Do Vision Language Models Struggle To Recognize Human Emotions?
items.abstract: This paper investigates why Vision-Language Models (VLMs) struggle with dynamic facial emotion recognition (DFER). The core finding is that VLM pre-training exacerbates the long-tailed nature of emotion datasets, causing them to misclassify rare emotions as common ones. The contribution involves proposing alternative sampling strategies to mitigate this head-class bias and improve VLM performance on subtle, under-represented emotional states.
items.figure_caption: Qualitative Failure Analysis of SOTA VLMs on Video Emotion Recognition. VLMs like Gemini2.5-Flash, Qwen2.5-VL and EmotionQwen misclassify emotions by failing to capture subtle, temporal cues. Examples include overlooking a brief smile (top row, ‘Happiness’ misclassified as ‘Sadness/Neutral/Disgust’) or a tensed facial expression (bottom row, ‘Anger’ misclassified as ‘Neutral/Anxiety’)

items.title: RadAgent: A tool-using AI agent for stepwise interpretation of chest computed tomography
items.abstract: RadAgent is a tool-using AI agent designed to generate Chest CT reports through a stepwise and interpretable reasoning process. Its core contribution is providing clinicians with a fully inspectable trace of intermediate decisions and tool interactions, moving beyond passive final outputs. This approach significantly improves clinical accuracy and robustness compared to existing 3D Vision-Language Model counterparts.
items.figure_caption: Overview of RadAgent . a Given a 3D CT volume and a user query, RadAgent first produces an initial report draft and then enters an agent loop guided by a clinician-inspired diagnostic checklist. At each step, the agent plans the next diagnostic action, selects an appropriate tool from a toolbox, updates its memory with tool outputs, and refines its findings until sufficient evidence is collected for generating the final report. b An illustrative example trace showing how the agent verifies preliminary findings through sequential tool calls and accumulates evidence into a complete report. c Training and evaluation pipeline, including composite reward design, clinician-reviewed checklist construction, GRPO-based agent training, and evaluation on internal and external chest CT benchmarks.

items.description: AI You Control: Choose your models. Own your data. Eliminate vendor lock-in.
items.summary: This repository, "sponsors/thunderbird," promotes an AI platform focused on user control, data ownership, and eliminating vendor lock-in by allowing users to select their preferred AI models.

items.description: AI that sees your screen, listens to your conversations and tells you what to do
items.summary: BasedHardware is an AI system that monitors your screen and conversations to provide real-time guidance and instructions on what actions to take next.

items.description: A lightweight, powerful framework for multi-agent workflows
items.summary: The `openai-agents-python` repository provides a lightweight, powerful framework for orchestrating complex multi-agent workflows using the OpenAI platform.

items.description: The GEP-Powered Self-Evolution Engine for AI Agents. Genome Evolution Protocol. | evomap.ai
items.summary: EvoMap/evolver is a GEP-powered self-evolution engine designed for AI agents, utilizing a Genome Evolution Protocol to drive continuous improvement and adaptation.

items.description: DeepGEMM: clean and efficient FP8 GEMM kernels with fine-grained scaling
items.summary: DeepGEMM provides clean and efficient FP8 General Matrix Multiplication (GEMM) kernels featuring fine-grained scaling for optimized performance.

items.description: 《动手学大模型Dive into LLMs》系列编程实践教程
items.summary: This repository provides hands-on programming tutorials accompanying the "Dive into LLMs" series, focusing on practical implementation of large language models.

items.description: Claude Desktop for Debian-based Linux distributions
items.summary: This GitHub repository provides a desktop application for interacting with Claude, specifically packaged for Debian-based Linux distributions.

items.description: An open-source remote desktop application designed for self-hosting, as an alternative to TeamViewer.
items.summary: RustDesk is an open-source, self-hostable remote desktop application providing a secure alternative to commercial solutions like TeamViewer.

items.description: Claude Code skill to support Android app's reverse engineering
items.summary: This GitHub repository provides a Claude Code skill designed to assist users with the technical process of Android application reverse engineering.

items.description: Enterprise Architecture Governance & Vendor Procurement Toolkit
items.summary: Arc-Kit is an enterprise architecture governance and vendor procurement toolkit designed to streamline and manage architectural decision-making and technology acquisition processes.

items.title: Scan your website to see how ready it is for AI agents
items.summary: The website "isitagentready.com" offers a tool to scan your website and assess its readiness for interaction with AI agents.
